name: Cape Kri
description: World-record fish-density dive — a single survey here counted 374 species in a single dive. Drift along the wall with schooling jacks, snappers, sweetlips, and a constant traffic of reef sharks. Currents shift fast; the dive briefing matters.

getThere.label: Flights to Sorong (SOQ) via Jakarta or Bali

siteSpecificGear.name: Reef hook
siteSpecificGear.reason: Current rips around the cape — hooking in is the only way to watch the schooling action.

siteSpecificGear.name: SMB + reel
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift exits over deep water. SMB is required.

notes: Down-currents reported by experienced divers. Stay with the guide; do not chase fish into the blue.

name: Blue Magic
description: Submerged seamount in Dampier Strait that pulls in oceanic mantas, mobula rays, and big-fish action. Top of the pinnacle sits at 7 m; the sides drop into blue water. Current is the price of admission.

siteSpecificGear.name: Reef hook
siteSpecificGear.reason: Top of the seamount is exposed to ripping current.

siteSpecificGear.name: SMB + reel
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift surfacings over open water.

name: Manta Sandy
description: Sand-bottom cleaning station at 15 m. Reef mantas line up at coral bommies while cleaner wrasse work them over. Easy dive — limited current, defined manta queue line you stay behind.

siteSpecificGear.name: Patience
siteSpecificGear.reason: Stay behind the marked queue line. Mantas leave if pushed.

name: German Channel
description: Cut-through channel dredged in 1908 connecting inner lagoon to outer reef. Mantas come in to be cleaned on the sandy bottom, and grey reef sharks patrol the channel mouth on falling tide.

siteSpecificGear.name: Surface marker buoy
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift exits in the channel — operators require SMB.

name: Blue Holes
description: Four vertical shafts in the reef table that open into a single cavern. Drop through the holes at 10 m, exit at 30+ m through the cavern window, then drift to Blue Corner. Beam-shafts of light through the openings make it one of the most photographed dives in the Pacific.

siteSpecificGear.name: Dive light
siteSpecificGear.reason: Cavern is dark below 20 m — even with surface light overhead.

notes: Most operators run Blue Holes into a Blue Corner drift — single tank, two iconic sites.

name: Bajo Alcyone
description: Submerged seamount 25 m below the surface, rising from the deep. Hammerhead schools in the hundreds patrol the up-current edge. Cold thermocline, surge, and current — the most famous dive in the Eastern Pacific.

getThere.label: Liveaboard departure from Puntarenas (~36 h crossing)

siteSpecificGear.name: Temperate-grade wetsuit
siteSpecificGear.reason: Thermocline drops to 19°C at depth even in season.

siteSpecificGear.name: Reef hook
siteSpecificGear.reason: Standard procedure: hook in at the up-current edge of the seamount.

siteSpecificGear.name: Nitrox certification
siteSpecificGear.reason: Most operators run nitrox for the long deep profiles required at 25–40 m.

name: Barracuda Point
description: Drift along the wall and a tornado of chevron barracuda forms overhead. Add turtles, schooling jacks, and the occasional whitetip shark. Often run as a single drift dive — current dictates direction.

siteSpecificGear.name: SMB + reel
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift exits over deep water are standard here.

notes: Permit-controlled — 176 daily permits for the entire island. Book the permit months ahead.

name: Shark Bay (Wolf Island)
description: Northern point of Wolf Island where hammerhead schools sweep through in the hundreds. Hook into the rocks at 18 m, watch the sharks pass at arm's length. Whale sharks in season; dolphin pods occasionally crash the dive.

siteSpecificGear.name: Coldwater wetsuit + hood
siteSpecificGear.reason: Humboldt upwellings drop temp to 19°C in season — third dive of the day is the cold one.

siteSpecificGear.name: Reef hook
siteSpecificGear.reason: Hooking is the only way to hold the line while hammerheads pass.

siteSpecificGear.name: Nautilus Lifeline or PLB
siteSpecificGear.reason: Surface separations have happened here. PLB is standard kit.

name: Shark Airport (North Atoll)
description: Reef plateau on the North Atoll where whitetip and grey reef sharks rest on the sand. Drift the wall and stop on top to watch the sharks come and go. Visibility consistently >30 m in season.

siteSpecificGear.name: SMB + reel
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift exits over deep water — required by all operators.

notes: Park is open mid-March to mid-June only — strict seasonal window.

name: Big Brother
description: Offshore pinnacle in the southern Red Sea with two wrecks on the plateau — the Numidia and the Aida. Walls drop into the blue where oceanic whitetips, threshers, and hammerheads cruise. Strong current makes it advanced-only.

getThere.label: Flights to Hurghada (HRG) or Marsa Alam (RMF)

siteSpecificGear.name: SMB + long reel
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift exits in open ocean — operators want long-line SMB.

siteSpecificGear.name: Dive light
siteSpecificGear.reason: Two wrecks on the plateau, both worth penetrating with a guide.

notes: Liveaboard-only access — no day-boat trips reach Brothers.

name: Fish Head (Mushimasmingili Thila)
description: Pinnacle in North Ari Atoll famous for grey reef sharks circling the top in current. The reef bristles with sea fans and overhangs from 8 m to 30 m. Drift on, hook in at the top of the thila, watch the shark traffic.

siteSpecificGear.name: Reef hook
siteSpecificGear.reason: Currents at the top of the thila are strong enough that hooking in is the only way to hold position.

siteSpecificGear.name: SMB + reel
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift exits over deep water — surface marker is required by all operators here.

notes: Best dived on incoming tide. Grey reef shark numbers peak in afternoons.

name: Maaya Thila
description: Iconic night-dive pinnacle in North Ari. By day it's a moderate reef circuit; by night the thila comes alive with hunting white-tip reef sharks, octopuses, and free-swimming morays.

siteSpecificGear.name: Primary dive light
siteSpecificGear.reason: The marquee dive here is at night — bring a focused beam, not just a backup.

notes: Night dive is the highlight. Day-dive only is missing the point.

name: Manta Alley
description: Cleaning and feeding station on the south side of Komodo where mantas come in to be cleaned by wrasse. Calmer than Batu Bolong, but a long boat ride from Labuan Bajo — usually a liveaboard stop.

siteSpecificGear.name: Camera (wide-angle)
siteSpecificGear.reason: Mantas can be close enough to fill the frame. Strobes optional in clear water.

notes: Liveaboard south-circuit stop. Day boats from Labuan Bajo rarely make it this far.

name: Castle Rock
description: Submerged pinnacle in Komodo's north — top of the rock sits at 4 m, drops to 40+ m. Strong current splits around the rock and concentrates schooling jacks, trevallies, and reef sharks on the up-current side.

siteSpecificGear.name: Reef hook
siteSpecificGear.reason: Top of the rock is exposed to ripping current — hooking in is the only way to watch the action.

siteSpecificGear.name: SMB + reel
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift surfacings put you over 40m of water — SMB required.

notes: Negative entry required. Don't surface up-current of the rock.

name: Manta Point (Madivaru)
description: Cleaning station on the eastern edge of South Ari Atoll where reef mantas queue at coral bommies in the afternoon. Outside the cleaning windows the site is a relaxed reef dive at 10-14 m; during peak activity it can feel like manta traffic control.

getThere.label: Seaplane transfer (~25 min) to South Ari resorts

lodging.label: MV Carpe Diem (liveaboard, Central Atolls route)

siteSpecificGear.name: Dive computer
siteSpecificGear.reason: Operators here expect divers to track their own NDL and surface intervals across multi-dive days.

siteSpecificGear.name: Surface marker buoy
siteSpecificGear.reason: Boats stack up during peak season; signalling on ascent prevents prop incidents.

siteSpecificGear.name: Wide-angle camera or GoPro
siteSpecificGear.reason: Mantas pass close enough that a standard phone case rarely captures the scale.

notes: Cleaning activity peaks at outgoing tide. Operators usually time the dive accordingly.

name: Batu Bolong
description: A single rocky pinnacle in the middle of Komodo's central straits. Currents split around it, leaving one side calm enough to drift the wall while the other rips overhead at three knots. Nutrient flow keeps the reef carpeted in soft coral and stacked with reef fish from 5 m to 40 m+.

getThere.label: Flights to Labuan Bajo (LBJ) via Bali (DPS)
Day boat from Labuan Bajo harbour (~2h to site)

siteSpecificGear.name: Reef hook with lanyard
siteSpecificGear.reason: Current can pin you to the wall — hooking onto dead reef saves air and posture.

siteSpecificGear.name: Heavier wetsuit (mid-year)
siteSpecificGear.reason: Southern Komodo upwellings drop the water temperature sharply in June–September. Layer up.

siteSpecificGear.name: SMB + reel
siteSpecificGear.reason: Drift exits put you in shipping channels. SMB up before ascent is non-negotiable.

siteSpecificGear.name: Dive computer
siteSpecificGear.reason: Deep, current-rich dives — accurate NDL tracking matters.

notes: Always brief the current direction with the guide. Wrong side of the pinnacle = blown off the wall.

name: Darwin's Arch (The Pillars)
description: Liveaboard-only seamount off the remote Darwin Island. Schooling scalloped hammerheads in the hundreds, whale sharks in season, silky sharks circling the surface. Strong current, surge, cold thermoclines, and the second-best shark-density site on the planet. The arch itself collapsed in 2021; the pillars remain.

getThere.label: Flights to Baltra (GPS) via Quito (UIO) or Guayaquil (GYE)
Liveaboard departure from San Cristobal (SCY) - Darwin is 30+ hours by sea

siteSpecificGear.name: Coldwater wetsuit + hooded vest
siteSpecificGear.reason: Cold Humboldt upwellings — hypothermia risk on the third dive of the day if you're under-suited.

siteSpecificGear.name: Reef hook with safety lanyard
siteSpecificGear.reason: Standard procedure: hook into rock, watch hammerheads parade past at safe distance.

siteSpecificGear.name: Nautilus Lifeline or PLB
siteSpecificGear.reason: Surface currents have separated divers from boats here. Personal beacon is essential.

siteSpecificGear.name: Air-integrated dive computer
siteSpecificGear.reason: Multi-tank cold deep dives — accurate gas/NDL tracking matters more than at a warm reef.

notes: Park-permitted operators only. Required certs: AOW + Nitrox + 50 logged dives at minimum (most boats want more).
